title: Buttery Pillowy French Fish Stew
description: A romantic Lyon-inspired fish stew, featuring cornstarch and a brine-dried element, this dish is perfect for a special evening with guests.
instructions: Rouge the cod fillets in lemon juice and a pinch of salt for 30 minutes, then pat dry.
In a medium saucepan, melt 25g of butter and mix with 1 tsp of smoked paprika.
Add the garlic fish broth to the saucepan and whisk to combine.
Bring the broth to a low simmer and cook until it thickens to a rich, pillowy roux.
In a large, deep saucepan or Dutch oven, heat 1 tbsp of olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the cod fillets and sear until golden on both sides, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Transfer the seared cod to a clean plate and set aside.
In the same saucepan, add the dried seaweed, cornstarch, and a pinch of salt.
Cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes, until the seaweed is fragrant and the cornstarch has dissolved.
Add the garlic fish broth to the saucepan, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the broth to a simmer and cook until it thickens to a rich, creamy consistency.
Stir in the remaining 25g of butter until melted and well combined.
Return the seared cod to the saucepan and simmer, gently, until cooked through.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve immediately.
Serve with crusty bread or over rice for a more filling meal.
